---
title: Cryptography and signatures
description: Hash data, verify signatures, and work with ciphertext primitives.
---

## Cryptography and signatures

[Open in Playground](https://playground.xelis.io/?%)

The standard library exposes hashes, signatures, addresses as public keys, and confidential-balance primitives.

```rust filename="crypto_tools.slx" copy
struct HashRpcEvent {
    blake3: string,
    sha3: string,
    mixed: string
}

struct SignatureRpcEvent {
    sender: Address,
    message_hex: string
}

struct CiphertextRpcEvent {
    commitment: RistrettoPoint,
    handle: RistrettoPoint
}

entry hash_note(note: string) -> Hash {
    let digest: Hash = Hash::blake3(note.to_bytes())
    let sha: Hash = Hash::sha3(note.to_bytes())
    let mixed: Hash = xor_hashes(digest, sha)

    fire_rpc_event(60, HashRpcEvent {
        blake3: digest.to_hex(),
        sha3: sha.to_hex(),
        mixed: mixed.to_hex()
    })
    return mixed
}

entry verify_from_sender(message: bytes, signature: Signature) -> u64 {
    let sender: Address = Transaction::current().unwrap().source()
    require(signature.verify(message, sender.to_point()), "invalid signature")

    fire_rpc_event(61, SignatureRpcEvent {
        sender,
        message_hex: message.to_hex()
    })
    return 0
}

entry encrypt_for_sender(amount: u64) -> u64 {
    let sender: Address = Transaction::current().unwrap().source()
    let ciphertext: Ciphertext = Ciphertext::generate(sender, amount)
    ciphertext.add(amount)

    fire_rpc_event(62, CiphertextRpcEvent {
        commitment: ciphertext.commitment(),
        handle: ciphertext.handle()
    })
    return 0
}
```

Use wallet RPC `sign_data` to produce signatures for `verify_from_sender`.
